popularStory.headline:
Samuel Hale
(2 July 1615 – 9 November 1693)
popularStory.description:
Baptized 1 July 1615 in Watton at Stone, Hertfordshire, England, Samuel Hale was at Wethersfield in the Colony of Connecticut in 1637. 

He was granted sixty acres of land for serving in the Pequot Wa …

zh.lifeSummary: 當Samuel Hale於1615年7月1日出生在英國英格蘭賀特福爾華頓 亞特 史東時，他的父親John Hale31歲，母親Martha Kirby30歲。他於大約1641年與Mary Smith結婚。他們至少是4個兒子和4個女兒的父母。他去世於1693年11月9日在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加康涅狄格州哈特福德維瑟斯費爾德享年78歲，埋葬在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加康涅狄格州哈特福德格拉斯頓波瑞。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Samuel Hale于1615年7月1日出生在英国英格兰賀特福爾華頓 亞特 史東时，他的父亲John Hale31岁，母亲Martha Kirby30岁。他于大约1641年与Mary Smith结婚。他们至少是4个儿子和4个女儿的父母。他去世于1693年11月9日在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加康涅狄格州哈特福德維瑟斯費爾德享年78岁，埋葬在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加康涅狄格州哈特福德格拉斯頓波瑞。

nameMeanings.name: Hale
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: topographic name for someone who lived in a (usually remote) nook or corner of land, from Old English and Middle English hale, dative of h(e)alh ‘nook, hollow’, or a habitational name from a place so named such as Hale in Cheshire, Hampshire, Lancashire, Lincolnshire, Holme Hale (Norfolk), Hale Street (Kent), and Haile (Cumberland). In northern England the word often has a specialized meaning, denoting a piece of flat alluvial land by the side of a river, typically one deposited in a bend. See Haugh . In southeastern England it often referred to a patch of dry land in a fen. In some cases the surname may be a habitational name from any of several places in England named with this fossilized inflected form, which would originally have been preceded by a preposition, e.g. in the hale or at the hale. This surname is also established in south Wales.
Irish: shortened Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Céile (see McHale ).
Jewish (Ashkenazic): variant of   Halle  .

variants.name: Halling
meanings: English (Gloucestershire): habitational name from Hawling in Gloucestershire or possibly from Halling in Kent. Halling was named in Old English as ‘family or followers of a man called Heall’; Hawling may have the same etymology or it may have meant ‘people from Hallow’ (a place in Worcestershire named in Old English with halh + haga ‘enclosure’), or ‘people at the nook of land’, Old English halh (see Hale 1).
German and Dutch: variant of   Helling  .
Swedish: cognate of Hallin , formed with the suffix -ing ‘belonging to’.

variants.name: Halker
meanings: English and Scottish: perhaps from an agent noun based on Middle English halk ‘nook, corner, recess’ (Old English healc, healuc, a derivative of healh; see Hale ), denoting someone who lived in or by a nook. This surname is now rare in Britain.
English: variant of   Hawker  .

variants.name: Hallam
meanings: English (North Midlands): habitational name from Halam (Nottinghamshire) or from Kirk or West Hallam (Derbyshire), all named with the Old English dative plural halum ‘(at the) nooks or corners of land’ (from Old English halh ‘nook, recess’; see Hale 1). The name may also be derived from Hallam in Sheffield (Yorkshire), sometimes called Hallamshire, named from Old English hallum ‘(at the) rocks’, the dative plural of Old English hall ‘stone, rock’, Old Norse hallr.
English: possibly a variant of Allam with prosthetic H-.

variants.name: Hales
meanings: English (widespread, especially in the southeast): from the genitive singular or nominative plural form of Old English halh ‘nook, recess’ (see Hale ).
Irish: when not of English origin, this may be a variant of   Healy   or   McHale  .

variants.name: Haugh
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Connor, Eamon.
Irish (Clare): Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó hEachach ‘descendant of Eochu’, a personal name which is probably a pet form of Eochaidh, Eachaidh (see Hoey ).

Scottish and English: from northern Middle English and Older Scots halh, haugh ‘a piece of flat alluvial land by the side of a river, typically one deposited in a bend’, also ‘river-meadow land’; either topographic for someone who lived near such a feature or habitational for someone who came from one of several places in Scotland and northern England called Haugh.

variants.name: Hallows
meanings: English: variant of Hale 1, a topographic name from Middle English hale, Old English halh ‘hollow, nook, sheltered spot’. The final -s may indicate an original plural, but in some cases it is likely to be excrescent, a post-medieval modification of the surname.

variants.name: Heal
meanings: English:

topographic or habitational name for someone who lived in or by a nook or hollow, or at a place called with this word (from Middle English hele, Old English heale, dative singular of healh ‘nook or hollow’). Hele in Somerset derives from this word, but the surname arises chiefly from one or more of the many minor localities named Heal(e) or Hele in Devon. In southwestern England and Kent this is an equivalent of Hale .
in the southeast Midlands of England, perhaps a variant of   Hell   1.

variants.name: Bagnall
meanings: English (Staffordshire): habitational name from Bagnall or Bagnall in Alrewas, both in Staffordshire, named with the Old English personal name Badeca, Baduca (from a short form of various compound names with the first element beadu ‘battle’) + Old English halh ‘nook, recess’ (see Hale ) or holt ‘wood’ (see Holt ). In the 16th century the name was taken to Ireland, where it became established in Leinster.

variants.name: Haile
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Ethiopian/Eritrean Lemlem, Senait, Bereket, Seifu, Abrehet, Amanuel, Berhane, Dawit, Dehab, Fekade, Fekadu, Hagos.

English: northern or occasional form of Hale , or a habitational name from a place called with Old English halh ‘nook, hollow’, such as Haile (Cumberland) or the frequent Hale (for example in Cheshire, Hampshire, Kent, and Lincolnshire).
English and Scottish: variant of   Hail  .

variants.name: Hail
meanings: English and Scottish: nickname from Middle English, Older Scots heil, hail ‘healthy’ or ‘health’ (Old Norse heill ‘healthy’). This surname may well have become confused with the much more frequent Hale or, especially in Gloucestershire, with Hailes . Alternatively, in some cases the name may derive from the Old English personal name Hægel, from hægel ‘hail(stones)’, which may have survived into Middle English.
South German: variant of   Heil  .

nameMeanings.name: Samuel
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Biblical name (Hebrew Shemuel), possibly meaning ‘He (God) has hearkened’ (presumably to the prayers of a mother for a son). It may also be understood as a contracted form of Hebrew sha'ulme'el meaning ‘asked of God’. In the case of Samuel the son of Hannah, this would be more in keeping with his mother's statement ‘Because I have asked him of the Lord’ (1 Samuel 1:20). Living in the 11th century bc , Samuel was a Hebrew judge and prophet of the greatest historical importance, who established the Hebrew monarchy, anointing as king both Saul and, later, David. In the Authorized Version two books of the Old Testament are named after him, although in Roman Catholic and Orthodox versions of the Bible they are known as the first and second Book of Kings. The story of Samuel being called by God while still a child serving in the house of Eli the priest (1 Samuel 3) is of great vividness and has moved countless generations. In England and America the name was particularly popular among the 16th-century Puritans and among Nonconformists from the 17th to the 19th century. It became fashionable again in the 1990s.

en.lifeSummary: When Samuel Hale was born on 1 July 1615, in Watton at Stone, Hertfordshire, England, United Kingdom, his father, John Hale, was 31 and his mother, Martha Kirby, was 30. He married Mary Smith about 1641. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 4 daughters. He died on 9 November 1693, in Wethersfield, Hartford, Connecticut Colony, British Colonial America, at the age of 78, and was buried in Glastonbury, Hartford, Connecticut Colony, British Colonial America.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Samuel Hale est né le 1 juillet 1615, à Watton at Stone, Hertfordshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, John Hale, avait 31 ans et sa mère, Martha Kirby, avait 30 ans. Il a épousé Mary Smith environ 1641. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils et 4 filles. Il est décédé le 9 novembre 1693, à Wethersfield, Hartford, Connecticut Colony, Amérique coloniale britannique, à l’âge de 78 ans, et a été enterré à Glastonbury, Hartford, Connecticut Colony, Amérique coloniale britannique.

de.lifeSummary: Als Samuel Hale am 1. Juli 1615 in Watton at Stone, Hertfordshire, England, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war sein Vater John Hale 31 und seine Mutter Martha Kirby 30. Er hat um 1641 Mary Smith geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 4 Söhne und 4 Töchter. Er starb am 9. November 1693 im Alter von 78 Jahren in Wethersfield, Hartford, Connecticut Colony, British Colonial America und wurde in Glastonbury, Hartford, Connecticut Colony, British Colonial America begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Samuel Hale è nato il il 1 luglio 1615 nel Watton at Stone, Hertfordshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, il suo padre, John Hale, aveva 31 anni e il suo madre, Martha Kirby, aveva 30 anni. Lui sposato Mary Smith circa 1641. Erano genitori di almeno 4 figli e 4 figlie. Morì il 9 novembre 1693, nel Wethersfield, Hartford, Connecticut Colony, British Colonial America, all'età di 78 anni, e fu sepolto a Glastonbury, Hartford, Connecticut Colony, British Colonial America.

ja.lifeSummary: Samuel Haleが1615年7月1日にイギリスイギリスハートフォードシュアイアワットオン アト ストーンで生まれた時，父John Haleは31歳，母Martha Kirbyは30歳であった。約1641年にMary Smithと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも4人の息子と4人の娘の親であった。彼は1693年11月9日にイギリス植民地アメリカコネチカット州ハートフォードウエスエルスフィールドで78歳で死去し，イギリス植民地アメリカコネチカット州ハートフォードグラストンブリーにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Samuel Hale(이)가 1615년 7월 1일 영국 잉글랜드 허트퍼드실르 왓튼 앳 스톤에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 John Hale(은)는 31세였고, 그의 어머니 Martha Kirby(은)는 30세였다. 그는 대략 1641년 Mary Smith(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 네 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1693년 11월 9일 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 코네티컷 주 하트퍼드 베더스필드에서 78세의 나이로 사망했으며 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 코네티컷 주 하트퍼드 글라스토버이에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Samuel Hale nasceu em 1 de julho de 1615, em Watton at Stone, Hertfordshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, seu pai, John Hale, tinha 31 anos e sua mãe, Martha Kirby, tinha 30 anos. Ele casou-se com Mary Smith aproximadamente 1641. Eles tiveram pelo menos 4 filhos e 4 filhas. Ele faleceu em 9 de novembro de 1693, em Wethersfield, Hartford, Conecticute, British Colonial America, com 78 anos, e foi sepultado em Glastonbury, Hartford, Conecticute, British Colonial America.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Samuel Hale родился 1 июля 1615 г. в местности ваттон ат стоун, хиртфордшир, Англия, Великобритания, его отцу по имени John Hale было 31 год, а матери, которую звали Martha Kirby, 30. Он женился на женщине по имени Mary Smith около 1641 г. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына и 4 дочери. Он умер 9 ноября 1693 г. в местности ветирсфиелд, хартфорд, Коннектикут, бритиш колоняль амирика в возрасте 78 лет и был похоронен в местности гластонбури, хартфорд, Коннектикут, бритиш колоняль амирика.
